entry.title: HW2
entry.content:
Static Personal Blog Website Project Report

Student Name: Zheng Chen
Student ID: ZY2557201
Submission Date: 2026-04-19

1. Website Information

ItemDescription
Website frameworkHexo 6.3.0
ThemeButterfly 4.9.0
Deployment targetGitHub Pages
Website URLhttps://corzanx.github.io/
Assignment 1 reporthttps://corzanx.github.io/2026/03/31/HW1/
Assignment 2 reporthttps://corzanx.github.io/2026/04/19/HW2/

The website is a static personal blog generated by Hexo. It can be accessed through the GitHub Pages URL above after deployment. The first assignment report has already been added as a blog post and is integrated into the same website.

2. Objective

The goal of this assignment is to set up and deploy a static personal blog website, manage the project with Git, integrate the previous Markdown report, and document the full process.

This project satisfies the requirements in the following ways:

  1. The blog is built as a static website with Hexo.
  2. The site is deployed to GitHub Pages.
  3. The previous assignment report, HW1.md, is included as a post.
  4. The project is managed with Git commits that represent different stages of development.
  5. This Markdown report documents the setup, deployment, Git process, and tool choices.

3. Project Structure

The main project files are organized as follows:

Important files and folders:

PathPurpose
_config.ymlMain Hexo configuration, including site URL and deployment settings
_config.butterfly.ymlTheme configuration for the Butterfly theme
package.jsonNode.js dependencies and build scripts
source/_posts/Markdown blog posts
source/_posts/HW1.mdAssignment 1 report integrated into the website
source/_posts/HW2.mdThis assignment report
public/Generated static HTML files
.deploy_git/Temporary deployment Git directory generated by Hexo deployer

4. Tool and Framework Choice

I chose Hexo as the static site generator because this blog had already been created with Hexo before, and the existing folder structure was still usable. Reusing the existing Hexo project made the process efficient and avoided rebuilding the website from zero.

Reasons for using Hexo:

  • It converts Markdown posts into static HTML pages.
  • It is suitable for personal blogs and assignment reports.
  • It has a clear project structure.
  • It supports themes and plugins.
  • It can deploy directly to GitHub Pages with hexo-deployer-git.

I used the Butterfly theme because it was already configured in this project and provides a complete blog layout, including post pages, category pages, tag pages, and local search.

The project uses the following npm scripts:

5. Setup and Recovery Process

Since this blog was originally created several years ago, I first checked the existing files instead of creating a new project immediately.

5.1 Check Existing Configuration

I inspected the main Hexo configuration file:

The important configuration values were:

This shows that the blog is configured to use the Butterfly theme and deploy to the GitHub Pages repository.

5.2 Check Existing Posts

The existing posts are stored in:

The first assignment report is:

Because Hexo uses the post date and title to generate the URL, the deployed HW1 page is:

5.3 Add the Second Assignment Report

I created this file:

The front matter defines the title, date, tags, and category:

After deployment, the expected URL of this report is:

6. Integration of Assignment 1

Assignment 1 is integrated into the blog as a normal Hexo post.

The source Markdown file is:

The public page URL is:

It is also grouped under the category:

This makes the first assignment accessible from the blog’s post list, category page, archive page, and direct link.

7. Git Management Process

The project is managed with Git at two levels:

  1. The Hexo source project is managed as the development repository.
  2. The generated static website is deployed through Hexo’s Git deployer to GitHub Pages.

7.1 Source Repository Commits

I used meaningful commits to record the development process. Each commit corresponds to one logical step rather than an unrelated small change.

StepCommit MessagePurpose
1Initialize Hexo source projectAdd the core Hexo configuration, npm package files, scaffolds, and ignore rules
2Add Butterfly theme configurationAdd the Butterfly theme configuration and record the theme repository reference
3Add existing blog source contentAdd existing posts, custom pages, and CSS files that form the blog content
4Add assignment 1 report postAdd HW1.md and integrate the previous assignment into the website
5Add assignment 2 deployment reportAdd HW2.md to document the static blog setup and deployment process

These commits reflect the logical progression of the project:

  1. Prepare the static site project.
  2. Configure the visual theme and keep the theme source traceable.
  3. Add the blog content.
  4. Integrate the previous homework.
  5. Document and complete the second homework.

7.2 Deployment Git Process

Hexo generates static files into the public/ directory. The deployment command is:

This script runs:

The deployer publishes the generated HTML files to:

The configured deployment branch is:

8. Build and Deployment Steps

The complete build and deployment process is:

8.1 Install Dependencies

If the dependencies are missing, install them with:

8.2 Generate Static Files

Run:

This executes:

The generated HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and search index files are written to:

8.3 Preview Locally

Run:

Then open the local preview URL shown by Hexo, usually:

8.4 Deploy to GitHub Pages

Run:

After deployment, the website can be visited at:

9. Accessibility and Functionality Check

I checked the project from the following aspects:

Check ItemResult
Home page generationPassed
Post page generationPassed
HW1 page linkPassed
HW2 page generationPassed
Category pagePassed
Tag pagePassed
Search index generationPassed
Static resource generationPassed

The website is static, so it does not require a backend server after deployment. All pages can be served directly by GitHub Pages.

10. Reflection

This assignment helped me reconnect with a Hexo blog project that had not been actively maintained for several years. Instead of discarding the old project, I inspected its structure, confirmed the deployment configuration, reused the existing theme, and added new assignment content.

The most useful part of the process was understanding the relationship between Markdown source files and generated static pages:

  • Markdown files in source/_posts/ are the source content.
  • Hexo converts them into HTML pages.
  • GitHub Pages serves the final static website.
  • Git records the development history.

This workflow is simple but practical. It is suitable for course reports, technical notes, and personal documentation.

11. Conclusion

In this project, I completed a static personal blog website using Hexo and GitHub Pages. I integrated the first assignment report into the blog, created this second assignment report, documented the setup and deployment process, and organized the project with meaningful Git commits.

The final website URL is:

The Assignment 1 report URL is:

The Assignment 2 report URL is:

References

  • Hexo Documentation: https://hexo.io/docs/
  • Hexo Deployment Documentation: https://hexo.io/docs/one-command-deployment
  • Butterfly Theme Documentation: https://butterfly.js.org/
  • GitHub Pages Documentation: https://docs.github.com/en/pages

entry.title: HW1
entry.content:
Matrix Multiplication Project Report

Student Name: Zheng Chen
Student ID: ZY2557201
Submission Date: [2026-04-03]

System Configuration

ItemDescription
CPU ModelApple M1 Pro
Memory Sizehw.memsize: 17179869184
Operating System VersionDarwin Corzans-MacBook-Pro.local 23.2.0 Darwin Kernel Version 23.2.0: Wed Nov 15 21:53:18 PST 2023; root:xnu-10002.61.3~2/RELEASE_ARM64_T6000 arm64
Compiler VersionApple clang version 15.0.0 (clang-1500.3.9.4)
Python VersionPython 3.11.3

Implementation Details

C Language Implementation

  • Source Code:
  • Compilation Command:
  • Execution Command:

Python Language Implementation

  • Source Code:
  • Execution Command:

Algorithm Verification

To verify the correctness of the matrix multiplication algorithm, we manually compute the result of multiplying matrices A and B.

Matrix A:

Matrix B:

Manual calculation:

Result:

The outputs from both C and Python implementations match the manually computed results, confirming the correctness of the algorithm.

Performance Analysis (Bonus)

Execution Times

Matrix SizeC Time (s)Python Time (s)
100×1000.0020780.157317
200×2000.0171101.693231
300×3000.0580424.182329

Deep Insights

  1. Time Complexity Growth (O(n³))
    The execution time increases rapidly as the matrix size grows. This is expected because matrix multiplication has a time complexity of O(n³). Doubling the matrix size results in approximately eight times more computations.
  2. Compiled vs Interpreted Execution
    The C implementation is significantly faster because it is compiled into machine code before execution. In contrast, Python is interpreted, introducing overhead at runtime for each operation.
  3. Memory Access and Cache Efficiency
    C provides better control over memory layout, leading to more efficient cache usage. Python’s high-level data structures (lists of lists) introduce additional overhead and less predictable memory access patterns.
  4. Loop Overhead
    Python suffers from higher loop overhead due to dynamic typing and interpreter checks in each iteration, whereas C executes loops much more efficiently.
  5. Scalability Observation
    As the matrix size increases, the performance gap between C and Python becomes more significant, indicating that C is more suitable for computation-intensive tasks.
  6. Potential Optimization Opportunities
    • Using optimized libraries such as NumPy in Python can drastically improve performance.
    • Applying advanced algorithms can reduce time complexity.

Conclusion

In this project, we implemented matrix multiplication using both C and Python, and explored the differences between compiled and interpreted languages.

We also gained practical experience with:

  • Unix/Linux command line operations
  • Markdown documentation
  • Program compilation and execution

The results show that C provides better performance, while Python offers easier implementation and readability.

References

  • Python Official Documentation: https://docs.python.org/3/
  • GCC Documentation: https://gcc.gnu.org/

Appendix

Additional Notes

  • The matrix sizes used in testing were relatively small for demonstration purposes.
  • Larger matrices can be used for more accurate performance comparisons.
  • Future improvements may include using optimized libraries such as NumPy in Python.

entry.title: 大学生该如何接触自媒体
entry.content:
自媒体是指个人通过互联网等新媒体平台，创作、分享和传播自己的内容，以吸引观众或读者的一种方式。对于当今的大学生来说，自媒体具有一定的吸引力和意义，但也需要谨慎对待和审慎选择。

  1. 创新和表达平台：自媒体为大学生提供了一个创新和表达自己观点、才华和兴趣的平台。他们可以通过博客、视频、社交媒体等展示自己的作品和见解，与他人分享经验和知识。

  2. 职业发展机会：自媒体可以为大学生提供潜在的职业发展机会。一些大学生可能通过自媒体获得广告收入、赞助和其他形式的合作机会，甚至可能将其作为未来职业的起点。

  3. 个人品牌建设：自媒体可以帮助大学生建立个人品牌，展示他们的专业知识、技能和特长。这对于毕业后找工作、创业或继续深造都有积极影响。

[{"url":"https://z1.ax1x.com/2023/09/16/pPfIao4.png","alt":""},{"url":"https://z1.ax1x.com/2023/09/16/pPfIYLT.png","alt":""},{"url":"https://z1.ax1x.com/2023/09/16/pPfINeU.png","alt":""},{"url":"https://z1.ax1x.com/2023/09/16/pPfIUwF.png","alt":""}]

  1. 责任和可信度：大学生在做自媒体时应该意识到他们的影响力和责任。传播信息要准确、客观、负责任，避免散播错误信息或误导观众。建立可信度对于长期发展至关重要。

  2. 平衡学业和自媒体：大学生需要注意平衡自媒体活动与学业之间的关系。学业是他们主要的责任和任务，不能因为自媒体而影响学术表现。合理安排时间，确保自媒体活动不干扰学术进展。

  3. 挑战和竞争：自媒体领域充满了激烈的竞争和挑战。大学生需要具备良好的创意、内容质量和推广能力，以在众多自媒体创作者中脱颖而出。

    [{"url":"https://z1.ax1x.com/2023/09/16/pPfIwFJ.png","alt":""},{"url":"https://z1.ax1x.com/2023/09/16/pPfI0Y9.png","alt":""},{"url":"https://z1.ax1x.com/2023/09/16/pPfIBWR.png","alt":""}]

总的来说，自媒体为大学生提供了丰富的机遇和可能性，但也需要具备扎实的知识基础、责任心和平衡能力。在自媒体领域取得成功需要持之以恒、不断学习和适应快速变化的新媒体环境。

entry.title: 23软外03班线上班会
entry.content:
线上班会

报到须知

1.9月10日-9.11日朝晖校区报到。统一从朝晖校区南门进入（新生报到的专用通道）

查阅学校地图

2.老校区停车位子有限，只有870个车位，除去教职工停的车，所剩车位有限，尽量乘坐地铁、公交等公共交通方式，停放完不要动。

3.凭入学通知书进校，步行至报到点，找到班级大本营，领取入学材料、文化衫等等。

开学前准备

1.确认数字迎新都已完成（尤其安全教育）

2.超星学习通平台课程（数学先修课必完成）

3.准备一寸证件照（白蓝底都准备一下），最好有洗好的，底片可以在学校照相店洗。

4.若需要申请助学金、转户口等，提前准备好相应材料

5.身份证，录取通知书，个人档案

6.思考要不要担任班干部（11号晚上）

(1)班长 (2)团支书 (3)学习委员 (4)生活委员 (5)体育委员 (6)心理委员 (7)宣调委员

可以提前准备的东西

1.接通知，入学后每一位新生需要写一份“个人自传”发给班主任，简单介绍一下自己的经历、内心想法和未来规划。

2.生活用品可以提前准备，学校里超市除了床上用品基本都有，可以到校买也可以自己带。

3.需要校园电话卡的可以提前办也可以到学校后再办，大概率是会有线下摆摊的。谨防诈骗。

迎新链接

报到指南(全面)

报到期间交通管理通告

安全教育入口

户口迁移

助学贷款办理教程

新生绿色通道

团组织关系转接

医保相关事项

新生体检须知

entry.title: C++ 数据结构 栈 中缀表达式转后缀表达式并求值
entry.content:
C++ 数据结构 栈 中缀表达式转后缀表达式并求值

这里用的是我自己写的Stack类，实现方法为静态数组，但使用过程中的函数方法一样（最好还是使用链表实现Stack，当时我记得我被扣了10分）

中缀表达式转后缀表达式：

1.从左到右遍历

2.数，即参与运算数，直接放进后缀表达式之后

3.左括号，直接压入栈（因为括号的优先级最高，无需判断）（入栈后优先级最低，确保其它的符号正常入栈）

**4.右括号,**意味着括号已经结束，那么不断弹出栈顶，直到遇到左括号。

5.运算符,将该运算符与栈顶运算符进行比较

​ 如果优先级高于栈顶运算符则压入堆栈

​ 如果优先级低于或等于栈顶运算符则将栈顶运算符弹出并放入后缀表达式中

 ***这里是核心，低于或等于栈顶元素意味着前面部分可以运算了，先放入表达式，即先进行运算的一定是高优先级的运算符。***

​ 直到优先级大于栈顶运算符或者栈空，再将该运算符入栈。

6.对字符串处理完毕后，则按顺序弹出栈中所剩运算符，放入表达式中

这里我给出一个例子：2+(3*(4-1))+3

注意，我最后；两步写错了，应该是2 3 4 1 - *  + 和 2 3 4 1 - * + 3 + /b>

code中的栈类是我自己写的类，不是STL中的，但功能大致一样，

后缀表达式的求值方法

1.依旧是从左到右遍历字符串

2.数，这次的数的处理是直接压入栈

3.符号，若是空格，则将前面保存的数压入栈（因为数可能是多位数，所以不能直接压入栈，有别于转后缀。所以我们需要一个数来保存字符输进来的数字。）

4.运算符，碰到一个运算符，我们就进行一次计算，对栈顶的两个数字进行计算，这也就是后缀表达式的计算原理

接下来举个例子，式子与上面的相同

后缀表达式为2 3 4 1-* 3++

最后所剩的14即为最终答案，直接输出即可

下面是实现的代码

Stack_static类的实现

全部代码及测试

entry.title: HomePod mini 初步使用体验
entry.content:
HomePod mini 初步使用体验

音质

700的音响也不能要求太多，低音有但不多，高音一股algorithm的味道，一分钱一分货，在700块绝对合格

最关注的延迟

apple自家一套🈚️延迟，Chrome看油管、b站、Netflix均有延迟，使用Safari就无延迟，对于习惯Google全家桶的我很蛋疼。

applemusic无延迟，Spotify、网易云、QQ等等均有延迟，纯看网络。

这点我就很奇怪了，既然都用算法音频了，干脆用蓝牙算了，何必使用Wi-Fi传输高保真音频呢？

定位

设计理念很好，定位更偏向于智能家居中枢，延迟真的有点蛋疼。如果有很多智能家居的话很适合购入，如果只是听个响，我更建议根据你的喜好购买更专业的，添点钱上曼哈顿、bose、马歇尔
